Tests division of `false` by a `Boolean` object resulting in `0`.

# Purpose
This code is a simple assertion test that checks the result of dividing `false` by a new `Boolean` object with the value `true`. The expression `false / new Boolean(true)` evaluates to `0` because `false` is converted to `0` and `new Boolean(true)` is converted to `1` during the division operation. The `assert` function verifies that the result of this division is equal to `0`. This code provides narrow functionality, serving as a basic test to confirm the behavior of type conversion and arithmetic operations in JavaScript.
